caseStudies.title: Asurion Tech Care
caseStudies.impactLine: Reframed connected-home protection from break/fix to a modular, customer-centered platform.
caseStudies.headline: Reinventing the connected home experience from the ground up.
caseStudies.hook: Customers saw the product as insurance they hoped never to use; we built a reason to stay every month.
caseStudies.summary: Led design vision for Asurion's flagship product transformation—from a single break/fix offering to a tiered platform informed by 500+ customers and quantitative validation.
caseStudies.problemFraming: See the full case study for problem framing, research, and outcomes.
caseStudies.roleAndTeam: Senior Director of Product Design; cross-functional design, research, and product partners.
caseStudies.approach: Workshops, qualitative sprints, conjoint validation, and in-field pilot research.
caseStudies.challenges: Legal, partner, and technical constraints while shifting to a product-led model.
caseStudies.solution: Modular coverage, clearer value narrative, and platform foundations for enterprise strategy.
caseStudies.resultsAndImpact: Strong preference for tiered choices vs. legacy; foundation for scaled partner rollout.

impactFallback.expectedMetrics: Share of preference, decliner rate, qualitative pilot feedback.
impactFallback.strategicImpact: Shift from client-led bespoke builds toward a shared product platform.
impactFallback.testResults: Conjoint and moderated research informed converged offering structure.

caseStudies.nextSteps: Deeper personalization, partner customization, and lifetime value metrics.

caseStudies.title: Asurion Fix-It Bar
caseStudies.impactLine: Shifted asurion.com from sales-first to customer-problem-first, increasing service initiations and out-of-warranty repairs.
caseStudies.headline: Turning a million monthly visits into a front door for tech help.
caseStudies.hook: Customers knew what their problem was—but the site made them figure out their provider first. We flipped that.
caseStudies.summary: Led the design of the Fix-It Bar on asurion.com—an intelligent router shifting 1M+ monthly visitors from provider-first to problem-first, validated through A/B testing.
caseStudies.problemFraming: 50+ fragmented partner portals, no unified authentication, and self-routing dead ends drove digital leakage to the call center.
caseStudies.roleAndTeam: Product Design Director; Sr. Product Designer, UX Engineer, Product Marketing, Engineering, Analytics.
caseStudies.approach: Vision storytelling, OKR alignment, conversational design model, iterative prototyping, CMS-powered MVP pivot.
caseStudies.challenges: Engineering constraints, security/hosting delays, unclear ownership, and a delivery-over-outcomes culture.
caseStudies.solution: The Fix-It Bar: a reusable, O360-integrated UI component making booking available on every page of asurion.com.
caseStudies.resultsAndImpact: Increased service initiations and out-of-warranty repair bookings; reduced digital-to-call leakage. Sr. Designer promoted to Principal.

impactFallback.expectedMetrics: Service initiation rate, out-of-warranty repair bookings, digital-to-call leakage.
impactFallback.strategicImpact: Established a reusable platform component and OKR framework adopted across teams.
impactFallback.testResults: A/B test of the full Fix-It Bar vs. CMS MVP confirmed the direction.

caseStudies.nextSteps: Nav-embedded booking, multi-service expansion, and behavioral routing intelligence.

caseStudies.title: Asurion & Xbox partnership
caseStudies.impactLine: Pilot support that beat CSAT targets, quant-backed tier benefits, and a lifecycle vision for embedding protection and help in Game Pass.
caseStudies.headline: Helping Xbox win by keeping gamers confident, engaged, and playing.
caseStudies.hook: From a human-triage pilot to research-backed vision demos—proving quality support opens the door to protection and lifecycle partnership.
caseStudies.summary: Led design strategy for Asurion’s Xbox partnership: pilot outcomes, journey audits, quantitative Game Pass benefit studies, vision and concept demos, and Asurion Care for Xbox product surfaces.
caseStudies.problemFraming: Xbox customers struggled to reach human help for hardware issues; the org needed proof of value and a credible path to deeper product integration.
caseStudies.roleAndTeam: Senior Director of Product Design; research, product, and partnership stakeholders across Asurion and Xbox.
caseStudies.approach: Experience audits, customer research, conjoint-style quant work with market research, clickable flow demos, and executive storytelling.
caseStudies.challenges: Partner alignment, AI-first support context, and embedding commercial offers without eroding trust.
caseStudies.solution: High-touch pilot, mapped lifecycle opportunities, tier-embedded benefits narrative, and technical integration maps with prototype demos.
caseStudies.resultsAndImpact: Pilot CSAT 96.6 vs. 75 goal, strong NPS, attach above goal, and research showing doubled premium upgrade intent with embedded benefits.

impactFallback.expectedMetrics: CSAT, NPS, protection attach, upgrade and retention intent.
impactFallback.strategicImpact: Foundation for expanding Asurion support and protection inside the Xbox ecosystem.
impactFallback.testResults: Pilot readouts and embedded-benefits quant study.

caseStudies.nextSteps: Game Pass tier integration, cloud onboarding support, and first-100-days lifecycle programs.

caseStudies.title: ExpertClaw
caseStudies.impactLine: An AI-orchestrated expert cockpit targeting sub-10-minute handle times while keeping the human expert in command.
caseStudies.headline: Customers deserve a human, not a search bar
caseStudies.hook: ExpertClaw cuts cognitive overload so experts can focus on empathy and speed—suggesting next steps while the expert delivers.
caseStudies.summary: Hackathon concept: an expert cockpit with real-time speech-to-intent, background tool execution, and next-best-action coaching in one surface.
caseStudies.problemFraming: 7M annual support calls, roughly 1M abandonments, and 24-minute average handle times with experts juggling too many tools.
caseStudies.roleAndTeam: Product Designer; engineering and product partners on the ExpertClaw team.
caseStudies.approach: Vision framing, cockpit UX, PRD co-authorship, demo narrative, and org readout.
caseStudies.challenges: Orchestrating many AI agents with governance versus one brittle omnibus model.
caseStudies.solution: Parallel automation layers feeding a single decision surface: intent, CRM, screen context, sentiment, and coached actions.
caseStudies.resultsAndImpact: Concept targets under 10 min handle time, trust-first upsell timing, and day-one expert confidence via guided flows.

impactFallback.expectedMetrics: AHT, FCR, conversion on post-resolution offers, pilot CSAT.
impactFallback.strategicImpact: Blueprint for agentic support that keeps humans in charge.
impactFallback.testResults: Hackathon demo and stakeholder readout; production pilots TBD.

caseStudies.nextSteps: Agentic knowledge base, revenue workflows, and live-volume pilots.

caseStudies.title: Asurion Field App
caseStudies.impactLine: Scaled a 5-star in-home service from MVP to 2 million jobs, sustaining a 4.91-star customer rating while growing 4x in two years.
caseStudies.headline: From proof of concept to enterprise platform.
caseStudies.hook: Two thousand experts in the field needed a tool that felt effortless. We built the platform that made it possible.
caseStudies.summary: Led design strategy for the Asurion Field App from founding concept through enterprise scale—enabling same-day in-home tech services across device repair, appliance repair, and Verizon device delivery.
caseStudies.problemFraming: Customers needed fast, in-home tech help. Asurion needed a platform flexible enough to support multiple job types while maintaining a 4.91-star experience at scale.
caseStudies.roleAndTeam: Product Design Director; founding designer scaling to domain lead over 3–5 product teams.
caseStudies.approach: Design sprints, in-field ride-alongs, qualitative research, A/B testing, and a phased pilot model.
caseStudies.challenges: Scaling a complex logistics platform while maintaining quality, onboarding new job types, and evolving team leadership.
caseStudies.solution: A unified expert app supporting device repair, new device delivery, appliance repair, and in-home tech support—all with real-time comms, inventory management, and CX rating feedback loops.
caseStudies.resultsAndImpact: 2M jobs in 2022 est., 4.91-star customer rating, 93% first-visit resolution, 19% VHDP take rate—highest across all Asurion channels.

impactFallback.expectedMetrics: Customer rating, job success rate, on-time arrival, VHDP attach rate.
impactFallback.strategicImpact: Established Asurion as one of the largest major appliance repair companies in the U.S. within one year of launch.
impactFallback.testResults: Pilot model validated each new job type before full rollout.

caseStudies.nextSteps: Optimize workflows for scale, expand job types, and achieve profitability by 2023+.

caseStudies.title: Asurion service design
caseStudies.impactLine: Blueprints, service standards, and a digital blueprint platform to align the enterprise on how the service actually runs—and where to invest next.
caseStudies.headline: Building the current and future Asurion service blueprint.
caseStudies.hook: Shared maps, measurable standards, and an AI-native tool turn tribal knowledge into something the org can steer.
caseStudies.summary: Program narrative: three compounding objectives, 2026 plan with service standards, production blueprint coverage, workshop outcomes, instrumentation, and the 5-star survey tied to standards.
caseStudies.problemFraming: Fragmented narratives about end-to-end service delivery across product, ops, support, and partners.
caseStudies.roleAndTeam: Service design leadership; cross-functional partners in supply chain, customer solutions, product, and marketing.
caseStudies.approach: Current-state blueprints, prioritization of broken windows, standards and metrics, design sprints, digital blueprint product.
caseStudies.challenges: Silos, inconsistent data handoffs, and the need for one system of record usable by executives and builders alike.
caseStudies.solution: Shared blueprints, published service standards, internal blueprint web app with AI assistant, post-service survey mapped to standards.
caseStudies.resultsAndImpact: Multiple production blueprints; systemic patterns surfaced; standards and metrics in review; tool used in executive and workshop settings.

impactFallback.expectedMetrics: Standard-level metrics, negative tag frequency by standard, blueprint health cadence.
impactFallback.strategicImpact: Single language for prioritization tied to customer promises.
impactFallback.testResults: Workshop validation and leadership deep dives on live artifacts.

caseStudies.nextSteps: Metric review with domain leadership, gap assessment, synthesis, medium-fidelity strategy.

caseStudies.title: AI-native service blueprint tool
caseStudies.impactLine: Structured blueprints in the browser—Executive and Enterprise views, in-product guides for non-technical readers, and an assistant that reads and writes the same data model.
caseStudies.headline: From static maps to a living system of record.
caseStudies.hook: Pain points, data maturity, media, and AI live on the map—not in a parallel doc.
caseStudies.summary: Internal Next.js / Supabase application replacing horizontal-only artifacts with multi-layer viewers, pain registry, rich props, threaded comments, role-based access, and a multi-provider LLM agent with scoped writes.
caseStudies.problemFraming: Three audiences needed different densities; Notion held pain data away from the map.
caseStudies.roleAndTeam: Product owner and design lead for internal service blueprint platform.
caseStudies.approach: PRD-driven domain model, Supabase RLS, Executive and Enterprise views with in-product guides, vision-to-import pipeline for legacy maps.
caseStudies.challenges: Keeping writes safe and consistent across agent and human editors.
caseStudies.solution: Tool-ordered agent loop, mutation refetch, typed annotations, data-flow matrix per step.
caseStudies.resultsAndImpact: Shipped internally; used in workshops and executive touch-wall sessions.

impactFallback.expectedMetrics: Authoring time, workshop time-to-capture, leadership review prep.
impactFallback.strategicImpact: Single structured artifact per journey for the enterprise.
impactFallback.testResults: Dogfood with service design and domain teams.

caseStudies.nextSteps: Expanded journey coverage, analytics on pain registry, tighter scorecards.
